---
title: 'Dear Her 05/13/2020 (Fasting Jen)'
date: '2020-05-14T04:06:00.0020000+00:00'
draft: true
blogger_blog: '(My Journal) Fed By Faith'
blogger_status: 'LIVE'
blogger_post_id: '2699741229759500873'
blogger_filename: '/2020/05/dear-her-05132020-fasting-jen.html'
blogger_source: 'takeout-20260223T235336Z-3-001.zip:Takeout/Blogger/Blogs/(My Journal) Fed By Faith/feed.atom'
categories:
  - 'Dear Her'
---

Dear Her,<div><br></div><div>I'm confused. Apparently, that's not a bad thing. I keep being told.&nbsp;</div><div><br></div><div>I don't like being confused. I like being the smartest one in the room and having all the answers. I like life fitting together neatly like a puzzle. I'm fully aware that life doesn't work that way, but, I want it to, damnit. ha ha.</div><div><br></div><div>I may have met you. Or not. Which is why I'm confused. If she's you, then you already know all of this, because we've discussed my confusion at length. And I'll be discussing with you all the things I heard and learned this week while we were apart.&nbsp;</div><div><br></div><div>For my own processing, and in case you're not Jen, and because if you are Jen, you'll smile reading this anyway... here's what I have as of Wednesday evening:</div><div><br></div><div>"She" didn't come in the package I expected. I know, I know. I was supposed to throw out that damned list, but, it's still in my head. Shelley fit that list and nearly ended my life... (felt that way anyway). So who cares about the list. I was supposed to throw it out anyway.&nbsp;</div><div><br></div><div>So here's this girl, who flat refuses to fit into the box. Any box. She's older than me, with kids younger than mine. Right off the bat, she breaks my paradigms there. It was a younger girl, young family, or older woman, both of near-empty nesters... she's neither and both. She's a vegetarian, who feeds her kids hot dogs. She's a lot of things, and none of them fit cleanly into my boxes, which Theresa thinks is hilarious! She's loving every minute of it. She's been trying to get me to stop putting people into boxes for a year and a half.&nbsp;</div><div><br></div><div>Here's where Theresa made an astute observation:</div><div><br></div><div><b>I can't even fit her into the "Wife" or "Not Wife" boxes, she won't fit into either one yet.&nbsp;</b></div><div><br></div><div>So I'm confused and frustrated!&nbsp;</div><div><br></div><div>Then again, if I knew she was my wife, then I'd just marry her. If I knew she wasn't, I'd fit her neatly into the friend's box. So... Here's the awkward middle where I have to get to know her without knowing the outcome, and that's scary.&nbsp;</div><div><br></div><div>Which leads me to Dr Jana, who said, that this whole middle-period, dating, seeing each other in the seasons of life, is what this building-it-right part is all about. The tension between is good, apparently. *rolls eyes*, ha ha. Jana said:</div><div><br></div><div></div><blockquote><div><i>"Confusion. Be curious about the confusion. Is this an attempt to control? I like the break for you to be curious about your anxiety and confusion but be careful about resolving the confusion rather than living in the tension of a dynamic world, with dynamic people, and a DYNAMIC God. I would like you to consider a goal of navigation rather than solving."</i></div><div></div></blockquote><div><br></div><div>Is this an attempt to control? YES! I need to know the future outcome before I start the adventure. I want the spoilers... And there it is. Risk removed if you know the outcome. But real risk is what the adventure is about. I have to invest in Jen without knowing if she's the one. That's scary! But exciting.&nbsp;</div><div><br></div><div>Jana also didn't balk at the married before God but not on paper, keeping separate houses for a while. She said the Bible doesn't dictate what most of that looks like, western tradition has. It could be "lying" to the government if we don't get married on paper, so we'd have to resolve that tension ourselves and present our case before God and whoever else we cared to discuss it with. But, that's a minor issue. The structure works. People are dynamic, relationships are dynamic, so this doesn't bother her as a Therapist or as a Christian Leader.&nbsp;</div><div><br></div><div>So... I wasn't expecting that. I was really expecting push-back on that. So... that's interesting.</div><div><br></div><div><br></div><div>Which leads me to Theresa's input:</div><div><br></div><div><i></i></div><blockquote><div><i>Do you really need to decide what she is right now? Do you really have to know? Or can you just... be? I can't think of a better example of learning people don't fit in boxes. Do you really have to put her into a box in your heart? Can you just let her live and be a part of your world and you be part of hers and see what happens? I'm not saying cut back on your interactions (unless you want to). I'm not saying you shouldn't date her. But can it just be a relationship and not an all or nothing "Your my wife or you're not" situation? I know it's difficult for you, because it will require you to live in a bit of tension, even if it isn't stresful tension. We've been raised in the church to only date for marriage. But you can also just date to know someone and see where it goes.&nbsp;</i></div><div></div></blockquote><div><br></div><div>Shandra also gave a pithy reply:</div><div><br></div><div><i></i></div><blockquote><div><i>I'm making the assumption that you and I are similar in the area of getting excited about hope and taking that baby Full Speed Ahead with 14 pairs of Rose Colored Glasses on; then, when we pause to look around there are so many scary flags flying everywhere, and with all those Rose Colored Glasses, all the flags are red. We can't decipher the reality that those may not be true, and it may also be because of the potential risk of being hurt again. So we just lump it all into one and decide it's either all good or all bad.... Also, "Don't marry the one that you love, marry the one that loves you", is the advice I was given.&nbsp;</i></div><div></div></blockquote><div><br></div><div>I asked Matthew and Karla Colonval to pray about it:</div><div><i><blockquote>Is there any 'but' about her? If there is a 'but', would you still want to marry her?</blockquote></i></div><div><br></div><div>There's a lot of "buts", but she's got Hp2, she's a vegetarian, she's this or that... and the longer I know her, <i>the less any of that matters</i>.&nbsp;</div><div><br></div><div>The longer I know her, <i>the stuff she really IS</i>, the woman after God's own heart, the safe space, the one who sees me, loves me, and will minister to my heart in ways I never could have imagined... who will minister to my sex life in ways I could never have imagined... I could see her being closer to my core than any woman I've ever known. I think that frightens me more than the "stuff doesn't match".&nbsp;</div><div><br></div><div>Why? Because, it will require I put to rest some of the old me, and rise up to become the man I always was meant to be. I'm going to have to level up if I'm going to be Jen's man. She is no bulldozer and certainly no doormat.&nbsp;</div><div><br></div><div>She is a woman of great strength AND great submission. I've never seen anything like it.&nbsp;</div><div><br></div><div>And that... is exciting! I was about to say scary, but, it's actually not. There was a day when I wouldn't have felt up to that task, but I've been slaying demons for several years now. The bigger ones are easier than the little ones back in the day. They're more obvious, and I have more help.&nbsp;</div><div><br></div><div><i>Side Note: If I taught men how to lead well and she taught women how to submit in strength... we could have a dynamic couple's ministry.&nbsp;</i></div><div><br></div><div>So? Yes. There are "buts" and I would marry her anyway. WOAH. I meant that. Wow. I don't know if I knew that, really knew it, until just now! Hm...&nbsp;</div><div><br></div><div>When Jen and I talked about all of this, she really is on board with just courting and seeing where it goes. I was hearing my own fear, anxiety, and pressure to fit it into a box and control the outcome, I wasn't really listening to her.&nbsp;</div><div><br></div><div>Luckily, I have most of it in text form. So I can re-read it now. She is telling me she likes not locking-in the future right now. She likes just being with me and seeing where it goes without pressure. Her heart is screaming not to let me go, marry me by July, but, she agrees with me that waiting for a longer period, courting, could be good. The cost may be too high to rush. So we take it one step at a time. Once we have firmer footing, take the next step. Once we're in each other's presence, we can decide from there. She's even okay taking a year or two to court first. It's me that's rushing this, not her.&nbsp;</div><div><br></div><div>She correctly identified a potential bait and switch inside of me. Laying on the "I want you" intensely, then backing off.&nbsp;</div><div><i></i></div><blockquote><div><i>"You get too deep. Offer entirely too much comfort and care. And women who are starved, activate the romantic relationship program. There is where you are sacrificing your wife. You are lying. It sweeps you away like an ocean. You anchor is not fast in the Lord".&nbsp;</i></div><div></div></blockquote><div><br></div><div>Also,</div><div><i></i></div><blockquote><div><i>"The longer you give the energy away that should be for your wife, the longer your commission will be back burnered. Simmering. Over boiling at times. I know you know this already. But I am telling you. Eyeball to eyeball..... As long as women are invited into your wife space, I mean deep-deep wife space, you will not get your wife. You will have a bunch of girls who are semi-gaga over you."</i></div><div></div></blockquote><div><br></div><div><br></div><div>Just to top it all off, this was the Chapter this morning in Creating Character Arcs:</div><div><br></div><div></div><blockquote><div>The Ultimate Choice between Want and Need</div></blockquote><div><i></i></div><blockquote><div><i>In plot terms, the Third Plot Point is all about creating a "physical" moment in which the protag's plot goals are endangered. In character terms, the Third Plot Point hinges upon not just "something bad" happening in the outer conflict, but rather an inner choice on the protag's part. At last, after two long story acts, he must choose between the Thing He Wants and the Thing He Needs - between the Lie and the Truth. Throughout the Second Half of the Second Act, he convinced himself he could have both. No, he realizes, that's impossible.&nbsp;</i></div><div></div></blockquote><div><br></div><div>She knew my Prophetic Nickname. "Dare" Darrell. There're too many of those moments to ignore this.&nbsp;</div><div><br></div><div>Hm......&nbsp;</div><div><br></div><div>As of right now, it's been:</div><div><br></div><div>03 Weeks</div><div>00 Days</div><div>00 Hours</div><div>21 Minutes</div><div>30 Seconds</div><div><br></div><div>Since I met Jeniffer Valentine Nelson/Bradford... I don't know who she is to me yet, but, I am VERY glad I met her. She's taught me more about myself, love, relationships, and marriage, intimacy, sex, manliness, leadership, etc... than any other single person. And she did it in three weeks. Imagine what she could do for me in a life-time?&nbsp;</div><div><br></div><div>There's a part of me that would walk away now before anyone gets hurt. Just walk away. Say "Hey, great to know you, no hard feelings". Shelley broke something in me, and I think it's that thing that has been keeping me from feeling the "in love" giddy feelings with Jen. Or, it could be that what we have is healthier, so it's growing deep first. I don't know why I don't feel those things yet. Last summer, I asked Jana and she said the real deal is different. You build it, you may not even recognize the person at first, may not even like them at first. Touche' Jana.&nbsp;</div><div><br></div><div>However, there's a MUCH larger part of me that says, if I had to choose now, I would marry her tonight. Nobody has ever loved me like this. I thought Shelley had, but, by comparison, Shelley was a child's playing house compared to this magnificent woman after God's own heart. If I had to choose now, I would say I will never find anyone who would love me this way ever again, I choose her.&nbsp;</div><div><br></div><div>And that's the catch, isn't it?&nbsp;</div><div><br></div><div>God told me he wouldn't tell me this time. I would choose. He'd have opinions if I asked him, but, it would be my choice, so that I would own the choice instead of feeling like it was thrust upon me, like last time.&nbsp;</div><div><br></div><div>If I had to choose now, I would choose her. But my heart warns me against that. I decide to walk away, my heart warns against that too.&nbsp;</div><div><br></div><div>So... The Tension. Apparently, I'm actually SUPPOSED to live inside this tension. Walk by Faith.&nbsp;</div><div><br></div><div>Images of "what if" or "how would it work" race through my mind... all without answers. There's no way to know any of that until we try to live it out. She's got to choose me and North Idaho for us to try. She doesn't love the cold and sunless north. So... we will see.&nbsp;</div><div><br></div><div>I choose the adventure. I choose the unknown. I choose to see what happens, without knowing where it will land.</div><div><br></div><div>Since I'm supposed to lead but allow the confusion, here's how I will navigate it:</div><div><br></div><div><b>First</b>, we will keep talking, courting, dating by phone. I will keep flirting, but, pull back on the overt phone-sex type engagement. That's inviting her into something I'm not ready for and making a false promise.&nbsp;</div><div><br></div><div><b>Second</b>, I will re-evaluate in a month, June 14th ish, and see where things are going, make adjustments at that point. By then, hopefully, she's on the road already. Enjoying her time with the kids, and that will change our available times as she'll be driving and can't text.&nbsp;</div><div><br></div><div><b>Third</b>, July. In July, sooner or later, she'll make it out to North Idaho. Or not. She may decide to skip Rainbow Gathering and do something else, but, she'll probably be here. I'll get into her presence and we'll decide what to do from there.&nbsp;</div><div><br></div><div><i><b>Note</b>: IF she's here and we're all on board, I'd like to spend time together as two families, Wolfe's and Nelson's, doing something. Maybe Wallace? Silver Mine's, Hot Springs, Etc. We shall see.&nbsp;Camping, or, Cabin? Maybe. Hm.&nbsp;</i></div><div><br></div><div><b>Four, who knows</b>.... if things go well in person, we'll keep up with the text/phone thing as she makes her way around the country. Then, it'll be up to her. Because if she's not moving here, I can't see myself taking it any further. I'm not leaving North Idaho anytime soon. I could see myself moving back to Indio someday, that has never left me, but, it doesn't fit today or this year. So then, some of the choices are up to her. She'll have seen the country, settled, and met me in person. We'll have had more interaction, seen the other sides of each other. She can pray about whether she can handle the dark winters here. If she moves here, I do intend to court her until we know one way or the other, exclusively. Time will tell.&nbsp;</div><div><br></div><div>#Selah</div><div><br></div><div>Darrell Wolfe</div><div><br></div><div>PS:</div><div>There a lot of little things....But it ultimately comes down to a few bite sized things...<br></div><div><br></div><div>#1. I need to come to terms with the fact that humans are just humans. Dynamic. There's a strong chance the thing I think I want either doesn't exist or is awful for me, and instead, I can just experience people until I find one I want to call home. Which is essentially what Dr Henry Clouds advice in his dating books always was. Toss the list, get to know real people.<br></div><div><br></div><div>#2. Real people, vs imaginary ones you made up, stress dynamic. They come as whole humans. Full of things. Mixed things. Some you'll like. Some you won't. Some, good and bad, you didn't expect. When you find one that presents a package worth fighting for, fight for it.<br></div><div><br></div><div><div>#3. I need to grow up.</div><div><br></div><div>3.a. That means not putting people in boxes, even the "wife" or "not wife" box. Just be. Experience. Let time tell which it is, don't rush to resolve that question. You could make a mistake either way.&nbsp;</div><div><br></div><div>3.b. It's time I stop collecting a harem. It's time I start investing my energy on wife level heart space only where I actually intend to cash in that investment. I've run myself thin with having a bunch of pseudo wives. Keep my friends, but, reserve wife energy for wife or serious contender committed relationship.</div></div><div><br></div><div>***</div><div><br></div><div>PSS:</div><div>Still waiting until Friday to talk to her?<br></div><div><br></div><div><div>Yes. Pressing in to prayer tonight and tomorrow. I want to ask a few things still....&nbsp;</div><div><br></div><div>Daddy:</div><div><br></div><div>#1. What do YOU want out of this budding friendship?</div><div><br></div><div>#2. How can I navigate this season and still honor you and her heart in the process?</div><div><br></div><div>#3. Reveal to me anything you want me to know about her or our time together so far or going forward.</div><div><br></div><div>#4. Is there anything else you want to add before I go back to her?</div></div><div><br></div><div>"""</div><div><br></div><div>PSSS</div><div><br></div><div><div>Dating. Is she the one? Is she not the one? I could be tossed to and fro, bounced around by the wind of emotion. But what if I had an anchor outside of either answer? What if I didn't have to know which she is and I could just experience her for who she is because I have an anchor in the God I serve, His will, His plan. And I submitted to His Will at each step.&nbsp;</div><div><br></div><div>Follow me, serious relationship, as I follow Christ. Let's see where HE takes us.</div><div><br></div><div>try to imitate me, even as I myself try to imitate the Messiah.</div><div>1 Corinthians (1 Co) 11:1 CJB, 1 Corinthians (1 Co) 11:5‭-‬8 CJB</div><div>https://bible.com/bible/1275/1co.11.1-8.CJB</div></div><div><br></div><div><br></div>
